assessment.title: JoraIQ Company-Based Assessment
assessment.description: Placement-readiness assessment covering hard DSA, 30-minute SQL, OOPs design and medium-to-hard Core CS MCQs.

questions.title: Incident SLA Scheduling with Dependencies
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
An enterprise support team handles incidents where some incidents can start only after prerequisite incidents are completed. Each incident has a resolution time and an SLA deadline. One engineer works on one incident at a time. The team wants to maximize how many incidents are completed within SLA while respecting dependencies.

Problem Statement
You are given n incidents numbered from 0 to n-1. dependencies[i] = [a, b] means incident a depends on incident b, so b must be completed before a. durations[i] is the time needed to resolve incident i. deadlines[i] is the latest completion time for incident i to be counted as on-time. You may resolve incidents in any valid order. Return the maximum number of incidents completed on or before their deadlines. If the dependency graph has a cycle, return -1.

open_test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

test_cases.purpose: First incident misses SLA but chain can continue
Dependent incident misses by one unit
Optimal order is not input order
Large dependent job may be skipped for SLA score
At least one SLA must be missed
Valid topo order with all on-time
Prerequisite can miss SLA but unlocks others
Small jobs before long job can still be optimal

questions.title: Same-Day Delivery Route Optimization
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A logistics company runs a same-day delivery system in a large city.

There are multiple delivery hubs and thousands of customer locations. Roads between locations have travel times. However, some roads are affected by traffic and can only be used after a certain time.

Each package has a pickup hub, a customer delivery location, and a latest delivery deadline.

A delivery partner starts from location 0 at time 0. The partner can carry only one package at a time.

For every package, the partner must travel from the current location to the package pickup hub, pick up the package instantly, travel from pickup hub to customer location, and deliver before or exactly at the deadline.

The partner may choose the order of packages. You need to find the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Problem Statement
You are given n locations numbered from 0 to n-1, m directed roads, and k packages.

Each road is [u, v, travelTime, openTime]. This means there is a directed road from u to v. The road can only be used at or after openTime. If the delivery partner reaches location u before openTime, they must wait until openTime. Arrival time at v is max(currentTime, openTime) + travelTime.

Each package is [pickupLocation, deliveryLocation, deadline].

The delivery partner starts at location 0 at time 0. Return the maximum number of packages that can be delivered on time.

Input Example
n = 6
roads = [[0,1,4,0],[1,2,3,0],[2,3,5,10],[0,4,2,0],[4,3,10,0],[3,5,2,0],[1,5,20,0],[4,2,6,5],[3,2,1,0]]
packages = [[1,3,18],[4,5,20],[2,5,25],[1,5,30]]

Expected Output
2

Note: roads are directed. The example includes road [3,2,1,0] so a route can continue from the first delivery area to another pickup hub. Without that directed connector, only one package is deliverable.

open_test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

test_cases.purpose: Sample with time-dependent roads and two deliverable packages
Must wait for a road to open
Late-opening fast edge loses to slower usable route
Cycle route can complete all packages
Delivery exactly at deadline is valid
Time-dependent shortest paths change with current time

questions.title: Global Backup Deduplication Range Analytics
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A backup platform receives files from many machines. Each file is represented by chunk hashes. Storage leaders want to answer audit questions: within a selected range of files, how many distinct chunks would be stored if deduplication were applied only to that range?

Problem Statement
You are given files, where files[i] is the list of chunk hashes present in file i. A chunk repeated inside the same file should be counted once for that file. You are also given queries, where each query [left, right] asks for the number of unique chunk hashes appearing in files left through right inclusive. Return one answer per query in the same order.
constraints: Total number of chunks across all files <= 200000

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

test_cases.purpose: Repeated chunk and empty range end

questions.title: Branching Versioned Key-Value Store
questions.prompt:
Real-World Scenario
A design platform allows users to create alternative versions of the same project. A new version can be created from any previous version. Each new version changes exactly one design parameter while inheriting all other parameters from its base version.

Problem Statement
Design a branching versioned key-value store. Version 0 is empty. set(baseVersion, key, value) creates a new version from baseVersion, updates key to value in that new version, and returns the new version number. get(version, key) returns the value of key visible in that version. If the key does not exist in that version, return "NULL".
constraints: Version numbers are created sequentially starting from 1
A parent-chain lookup solution is acceptable for this version
questions.expected_time_complexity: O(depth) per get, O(1) per set

open_test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates

hidden_test_cases.purpose: Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

test_cases.purpose: Sibling branches do not share later updates
Branch isolation with inherited root value
Two branches overwrite same key differently
Child update not visible in parent

questions.title: Delivery Promise Breach Analysis
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
An online retailer promises a delivery timestamp for every customer order. One order may be shipped in multiple packages. For customer experience reporting, an order should be treated as late only when the final delivered package arrives after the promised timestamp.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag High-Risk Customer Experience Cases. A customer should be flagged if: (1) they have at least 3 delivered, non-cancelled orders in the last 90 days, and (2) at least 40% of those orders were delivered late. Use the final delivered package timestamp at order level. Return only flagged customers.

Required Output
Return customer_id, customer_name, customer_segment, delivered_orders, late_orders, late_order_percentage, total_delivered_order_value, and last_late_order_date.
expected_approach: Use MAX(delivered_at) as final delivery time
Apply HAVING filters for minimum orders and late percentage

questions.title: Backup Failure Streak Risk Analysis
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
A backup platform runs scheduled backup jobs for many client servers. The operations team wants to identify active servers whose latest backup health is risky. A server is risky when the most recent backup attempts are failing repeatedly.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to return active servers where the latest 3 completed backup jobs are all FAILED. Ignore inactive servers and inactive policies. For each risky server, return the first failure date among those latest 3 failed jobs, the latest failure date, failure count, latest status, and latest error code.

Required Output
Return client_id, client_name, server_id, server_name, policy_name, latest_failure_streak_start_date, latest_failure_streak_end_date, consecutive_failure_runs, latest_backup_status, and latest_error_code.
expected_approach: Filter active servers and active policies
Rank completed backup jobs by server using ROW_NUMBER
Group by server and require all 3 jobs to be FAILED
Return latest error code from the most recent failed job

questions.title: License Utilization Review
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
A software subscription business sells seat-based licenses to customers. Some customers buy many seats but only a few assigned users actively use the product. Customer success wants to identify accounts that may need license optimization support before renewal.

Task / Business Rules
Write a SQL query to flag customers for License Utilization Review if: (1) they have an active paid contract with at least 10 purchased seats, and (2) fewer than 50% of purchased seats had valid product usage in the last 30 days. Exclude trial contracts and internal/test users. Count usage only for users who had an assigned license on the usage date. Customers with zero valid usage in the last 30 days should also be flagged.

Required Output
Return customer_id, customer_name, industry, plan_type, purchased_seats, assigned_users, active_licensed_users, seat_utilization_percentage, and review_reason.
expected_approach: Filter active paid contracts with at least 10 seats
LEFT JOIN valid usage from last 30 days
Flag customers where active licensed users are less than 50% of purchased seats or zero

questions.title: ServiceNow Incident Workflow Engine
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
ServiceNow handles different types of IT service tickets such as Incident, Service Request, Change Request and Problem Ticket. Each ticket type has a different workflow. The company wants to build a workflow engine where new ticket types can be added in the future without changing the existing workflow execution logic.

Problem Statement
The system should support creating different ticket types, moving each ticket to its next valid state, preventing invalid transitions, returning the current state of a ticket, and adding a new ticket type without changing existing core engine code.

domain_rules: Incident tickets follow New -> In Progress -> Resolved -> Closed.
Service Requests follow Draft -> Submitted -> Approved -> Fulfilled -> Closed.
Change Requests require Approval before Implementation and Review before Closed.
Problem Tickets support Investigation -> Root Cause Identified -> Workaround Provided -> Closed.
Invalid transitions must be rejected with a clear error or failed result.
required_components: A common Ticket abstraction or interface
Separate workflow/state behavior per ticket type
A WorkflowEngine or service that delegates transition rules
A way to query current ticket state
A response object or exception strategy for invalid transitions
design_constraints: Adding a new ticket type should not require editing existing engine transition code.
Avoid large if/else or switch blocks on ticket type inside the core engine.
Keep ticket data encapsulated; external code should not mutate state directly.
acceptance_signals: Uses State, Strategy, or equivalent polymorphic design.
Demonstrates at least two ticket workflows with different transitions.
Shows how a new workflow can be registered or introduced with minimal change.
Separates workflow rules from ticket identity and storage.
red_flags: Single procedural function with hard-coded ticket type checks
Public mutable state with no transition validation
Design cannot add a new workflow without modifying existing workflow engine logic

starter_code.python: # Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.javascript: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.java: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.
starter_code.cpp: // Design classes/interfaces for the workflow engine.

questions.title: Amazon Multi-Mode Payment Processing System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Amazon supports different payment modes such as Credit Card, Debit Card, UPI, Wallet, Gift Card and Cash on Delivery. Each payment mode has different rules. The system should allow new modes like BNPL, EMI or International Card without changing the core checkout flow.

Problem Statement
The system should support processing payment using different payment modes, validating payment before processing, returning payment success or failure, applying payment-specific rules, and adding new payment modes without modifying checkout logic.

domain_rules: Credit Card and Debit Card require card validation and bank authorization.
UPI requires VPA validation and payment confirmation.
Wallet requires sufficient wallet balance before debit.
Gift Card applies available gift balance before remaining payment.
Cash on Delivery can be accepted only for eligible orders and serviceable locations.
required_components: A PaymentMethod interface or abstract base
Concrete payment implementations for at least three modes
CheckoutService depending on the abstraction rather than concrete classes
Validation and processing methods with success/failure result
A factory, registry, or dependency injection approach to choose payment mode
design_constraints: Adding BNPL, EMI, or International Card should not require modifying CheckoutService.
Do not place all payment rules inside one if/else chain.
Payment-specific validation should live with the payment strategy.
Checkout flow should orchestrate payment without knowing internal mode rules.
acceptance_signals: Demonstrates polymorphic process or pay method.
Returns consistent payment result objects or status values.
Handles validation failure separately from payment processing failure.
red_flags: CheckoutService switches on payment type for every rule
New payment mode requires editing existing checkout logic
Validation and processing responsibilities are mixed across unrelated classes

starter_code.python: # Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.javascript: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.java: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.
starter_code.cpp: // Design payment abstractions and checkout flow.

questions.title: Autodesk Design Export System
questions.prompt:
Business Scenario
Autodesk tools allow users to export design files into PDF, DWG, DXF, STL, OBJ and PNG. Each format has different export behaviour. In the future, Autodesk may add SVG, IFC, STEP or GLTF. The system should allow adding new export formats without modifying the core design file class.

Problem Statement
The system should support exporting a design file into different formats, each format having its own logic, returning success/failure response, handling unsupported formats and adding new export formats without changing existing design file logic.

domain_rules: PDF export should support document metadata and page setup.
DWG and DXF exports should preserve CAD layers and units.
STL and OBJ exports should handle 3D mesh data.
PNG export should support raster resolution settings.
Unsupported formats must return a controlled failure instead of crashing.
required_components: A DesignFile domain object that does not implement every format directly
An Exporter interface or abstract base
Concrete exporters for at least three formats
An ExportService, registry, or factory for selecting exporters
A standard export result with status, format, and message or output path
design_constraints: Adding SVG, IFC, STEP, or GLTF should require adding a new exporter, not modifying DesignFile.
Avoid format-specific if/else chains in the DesignFile class.
Keep export settings explicit rather than hidden global state.
Separate validation of unsupported formats from export execution.
acceptance_signals: Uses composition so DesignFile delegates export behavior.
Shows concrete exporter classes with different behavior.
Provides a clear extension point for future formats.
Handles success and unsupported-format failure consistently.
red_flags: DesignFile has one method with switch/if branches for all formats
Adding a new format requires changing existing DesignFile logic

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A checkout platform supports card, wallet, BNPL and gift-card payments. Each payment mode has different validation, authorization, capture, refund and retry behavior. New modes are added every quarter by different teams. Which design best minimizes modification to checkout orchestration while still supporting mode-specific behavior?

options.label: A
options.text: A PaymentStrategy interface per mode, selected through a factory/registry, with checkout depending only on the abstraction

options.label: B
options.text: A single PaymentService with one if-else block for validation, another for refund and another for retry

options.label: C
options.text: A PaymentDTO with optional fields for every possible payment mode and validation inside the controller

options.label: D
options.text: A database table storing payment_mode and a shared generic method that assumes all modes behave identically

questions.explanation: Strategy plus registry keeps the orchestration closed for modification while allowing each payment mode to own validation, refund and retry behavior.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A service ticket has states New, Assigned, InProgress, WaitingForCustomer, Resolved and Closed. Each state allows different actions and some transitions require audit logs. The team currently has transition checks scattered across controllers, cron jobs and APIs. Which redesign is strongest?

options.label: A
options.text: Keep status as a string and document valid transitions in a wiki

options.label: B
options.text: Use State pattern or a transition table/service that centralizes valid transitions and side effects

options.label: C
options.text: Allow every service to update status directly and detect invalid transitions in reports

options.label: D
options.text: Move all transition logic to frontend button visibility only

questions.explanation: The core issue is scattered transition logic. Centralizing transitions through State pattern or a transition service reduces invalid transitions and duplicated rules.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A reporting service exports PDF today and will soon support XLSX, JSON and XML. The orchestration also handles authorization, data fetching and audit logging. Which design best applies Open/Closed Principle without duplicating common orchestration steps?

options.label: A
options.text: Create an Exporter interface for format-specific rendering and inject/select implementations inside a stable orchestration flow

options.label: B
options.text: Copy the entire export method once per format so each format is fully independent

options.label: C
options.text: Add format-specific if-else blocks inside the orchestration whenever a new format is requested

options.label: D
options.text: Expose raw database rows and let every caller implement its own export logic

questions.explanation: Common orchestration should remain stable while format-specific rendering varies behind an interface.

questions.title: OOPs
questions.prompt: A CheckoutManager validates coupons, computes tax, reserves inventory, charges payment, sends invoice email and writes analytics events. Any small change causes unrelated bugs. Which principle is most directly violated and what is the best correction?

options.label: A
options.text: Single Responsibility Principle; split responsibilities into cohesive collaborators coordinated by checkout workflow

options.label: B
options.text: Encapsulation; make all fields public so services can update them directly

options.label: C
options.text: Inheritance; create one subclass for every city and payment mode combination

options.label: D
options.text: Polymorphism; replace all interfaces with static utility methods

questions.explanation: The class has many reasons to change. SRP suggests separating tax, inventory, payment, notification and analytics responsibilities.

questions.title: Computer Networks
questions.prompt: Users can open a service using its IP address, but the domain intermittently resolves to an old IP for some users after a migration. HTTP health checks pass on the new server. Which diagnosis is most likely?

options.label: A
options.text: TCP congestion control is dropping packets after the TLS handshake

options.label: B
options.text: DNS caching/TTL or stale authoritative/recursive resolver records are still returning the old address

options.label: C
options.text: The application server cannot parse HTTP headers

options.label: D
options.text: The browser cannot render HTML returned from the server

questions.explanation: Successful access by IP but inconsistent domain resolution points to DNS propagation, caching or stale records rather than HTTP or rendering.

questions.title: Computer Networks
questions.prompt: A multiplayer game sends player positions 30 times per second. Old position packets are useless once newer packets arrive. The system prefers low latency over guaranteed delivery. Which transport choice and reason are most appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: TCP, because retransmission of every lost packet improves real-time freshness

options.label: B
options.text: UDP, because the app can tolerate loss and avoid head-of-line blocking from retransmissions

options.label: C
options.text: HTTP/1.1, because request-response semantics are required for every position update

options.label: D
options.text: SMTP, because it guarantees eventual message delivery

questions.explanation: For real-time updates where freshness matters more than completeness, UDP avoids retransmission delays and head-of-line blocking.

questions.title: Computer Networks
questions.prompt: A load balancer marks an instance healthy because the process responds to /ping, but users routed to that instance fail checkout because it cannot connect to the database. What is the best improvement?

options.label: A
options.text: Increase DNS TTL so users remain attached to one instance longer

options.label: B
options.text: Use a deeper health check that validates critical dependencies or expose a readiness check separate from liveness

options.label: C
options.text: Disable health checks and rely only on CPU metrics

options.label: D
options.text: Route all traffic to the instance with the lowest process uptime

questions.explanation: A liveness check only proves the process is up. A readiness/dependency check better indicates whether the instance can serve real user traffic.

questions.title: Operating System
questions.prompt: Three services acquire locks on Account, Wallet and Ledger in different orders. Production occasionally freezes with each thread waiting for a lock held by another. Which change most directly prevents this class of deadlock?

options.label: A
options.text: Use a global lock ordering rule and require all code paths to acquire locks in that order

options.label: B
options.text: Increase thread priority for the oldest request

options.label: C
options.text: Increase heap memory so locks can be stored faster

options.label: D
options.text: Move logs to a different disk partition

questions.explanation: Circular wait is a necessary deadlock condition. A consistent global lock ordering breaks circular wait.

questions.title: Operating System
questions.prompt: A batch job becomes extremely slow when its working set exceeds physical RAM. CPU utilization drops, disk I/O spikes and page faults increase sharply. What is the most accurate explanation?

options.label: A
options.text: CPU cache associativity improved but reduced instruction-level parallelism

options.label: B
options.text: Thrashing: the process spends excessive time paging data between RAM and disk

options.label: C
options.text: Deadlock: all threads are waiting on each other forever

options.label: D
options.text: DNS resolution failure between the process and database

questions.explanation: High page faults, low CPU and high disk I/O when memory is exceeded is classic thrashing.

questions.title: Operating System
questions.prompt: Two threads execute count = count + 1 on a shared integer one million times each. The final value is sometimes less than two million. Which explanation is correct?

options.label: A
options.text: The operation is not atomic; read-modify-write interleavings cause lost updates without synchronization

options.label: B
options.text: The compiler always executes both loops sequentially, so the result must be exact

options.label: C
options.text: The OS intentionally removes increments to improve performance

options.label: D
options.text: Integer addition is impossible in multi-threaded programs

questions.explanation: Increment involves read, add and write. Without a lock/atomic operation, interleavings can overwrite updates.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A service has low average CPU but high p95 latency because requests are blocked on database calls. Autoscaling only on CPU does not help. Which scaling signal is more appropriate?

options.label: A
options.text: Average CPU only, because latency cannot be used for scaling decisions

options.label: B
options.text: Request queue length, concurrency, p95 latency or custom application metrics tied to saturation

options.label: C
options.text: Number of source-code files in the repository

options.label: D
options.text: Amount of unused disk space on developer laptops

questions.explanation: When the bottleneck is waiting/queueing rather than CPU, scaling signals should reflect request saturation or latency.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A critical app runs on one VM in one availability zone and has nightly backups. Which risk remains even if backups are successful?

options.label: A
options.text: Backups remove all single points of failure

options.label: B
options.text: An AZ or VM failure can still cause downtime because backups help recovery but do not provide active high availability

options.label: C
options.text: High availability is guaranteed as long as the VM has enough disk

options.label: D
options.text: Adding more log files automatically creates failover

questions.explanation: Backups support recovery after failure; they do not prevent downtime from a single active instance or single-zone deployment.

questions.title: Cloud Computing
questions.prompt: A data platform stores millions of product images and event logs. Access is mostly by key/object name, cost matters, and POSIX file semantics are not required. Which storage type is the best fit?

options.label: A
options.text: Object storage, because it is scalable, durable and optimized for key-based blob access

options.label: B
options.text: Block storage attached to one VM, because it automatically scales globally without limits

options.label: C
options.text: CPU cache, because it is durable across restarts

options.label: D
options.text: A single local folder on one application server

questions.explanation: Object storage is designed for large-scale durable storage of blobs accessed by object keys.

questions.title: Network Security
questions.prompt: Employees receive convincing fake vendor-invoice emails linking to a login page that captures passwords. Which combined control is strongest for reducing both delivery and account-takeover risk?

options.label: A
options.text: User training only, because technical controls cannot reduce phishing risk

options.label: B
options.text: Email authentication/filtering, safe-link scanning, MFA and user reporting/training

options.label: C
options.text: Disable password complexity rules and reuse one shared password

options.label: D
options.text: Allow login only from browsers with dark mode enabled

questions.explanation: Phishing defense is layered: reduce malicious delivery, block/inspect links, make stolen passwords less useful through MFA and train/report.

questions.title: Network Security
questions.prompt: An API builds SQL by concatenating user input into a WHERE clause. Attackers can change query logic through crafted input. What is the best primary fix?

options.label: A
options.text: Use parameterized queries/prepared statements so user input is bound as data, not executable SQL

options.label: B
options.text: Hide error messages but keep concatenating strings

options.label: C
options.text: Ask users not to type special characters

options.label: D
options.text: Move the SQL string to a different source-code file

questions.explanation: Parameterized queries separate SQL structure from user values and are the primary defense against SQL injection.

questions.title: Network Security
questions.prompt: A dashboard service account only needs to read monthly sales tables, but it currently has permissions to drop tables and create users in production. Which security principle is violated?

options.label: A
options.text: Least privilege, because the account has more permissions than required for its job

options.label: B
options.text: Eventual consistency, because reads may be delayed

options.label: C
options.text: Polymorphism, because the account has multiple behaviors

options.label: D
options.text: Caching, because the dashboard may store results

questions.explanation: Least privilege means granting only the permissions necessary for the specific task.

questions.title: Computer Architecture
questions.prompt: A product-price service caches prices for 24 hours. The database updates prices hourly, and users sometimes see outdated prices after a promotion change. Which trade-off is being mishandled?

options.label: A
options.text: Cache freshness versus latency/load reduction; invalidation or shorter TTL may be needed

options.label: B
options.text: CPU instruction pipelining versus branch prediction

options.label: C
options.text: DNS recursion versus TCP retransmission

options.label: D
options.text: Disk formatting versus monitor refresh rate

questions.explanation: Caching improves latency and reduces load but risks stale data. Price data often needs invalidation or shorter TTL.

questions.title: Computer Architecture
questions.prompt: In a CPU pipeline, instruction I2 needs a value produced by immediately preceding instruction I1. Without forwarding, I2 may read the old value unless it waits. What type of hazard is this?

options.label: A
options.text: Data hazard, specifically a read-after-write dependency

options.label: B
options.text: Structural hazard caused by two monitors sharing one cable

options.label: C
options.text: Control hazard caused only by DNS redirects

options.label: D
options.text: Cache eviction policy violation

questions.explanation: I2 depends on the result written by I1, so this is a RAW data hazard.

questions.title: MS Excel
questions.prompt: A VLOOKUP formula returns wrong values after a new column is inserted into the lookup table because the formula used a fixed column index. What is the most robust correction?

options.label: A
options.text: Use XLOOKUP or INDEX-MATCH with explicit lookup/return ranges instead of a fragile hard-coded column index

options.label: B
options.text: Change the worksheet zoom level

options.label: C
options.text: Sort the sheet by font color

options.label: D
options.text: Convert all numbers to text without changing the formula

questions.explanation: XLOOKUP or INDEX-MATCH can reference the return column directly, reducing breakage from inserted columns.

questions.title: MS Excel
questions.prompt: A pivot table shows inflated revenue after orders are joined to order_items. Each order-level revenue value is repeated once per item row. What is the best analytical fix before creating the pivot?

options.label: A
options.text: Aggregate or deduplicate to the correct grain, such as one row per order, before summing order-level revenue

options.label: B
options.text: Change the pivot chart color to make duplicates less visible

options.label: C
options.text: Use COUNT instead of SUM for all financial metrics

options.label: D
options.text: Hide the duplicated rows manually without checking grain

questions.explanation: The issue is grain mismatch. Order-level metrics must be aggregated at order grain before joining or summing in a pivot.
